item.title: 10 Mind-Blowing Physics Facts
item.description: Ten incredible physics facts that will change how you see the world — from time dilation to neutron stars.

item.title: Atom the Mouse and the World Inside Everything
item.description: Atom the Mouse shrinks down to explore the bizarre quantum world where particles can be in two places at once and nothing is quite what it seems.

item.title: Balancing Act
item.description: Explore torque and balance by placing objects on a seesaw — can you predict what will tip?

item.title: Circuit Construction Kit
item.description: Build circuits with batteries, resistors, light bulbs, and switches to explore electricity.

item.title: Echo the Dolphin and the Invisible Symphony
item.description: Dive deep with Echo the Dolphin into the world of sound, vibration, and the invisible waves that carry music, speech, and whale songs.

item.title: Energy Skate Park
item.description: Build a skate track and watch energy transform between kinetic and potential as a skater rides the rails.

item.title: Forces and Motion: Basics
item.description: Push and pull objects to explore forces, mass, and friction with this PhET simulation.

item.title: Gigi the Giraffe and the Mystery of Why Things Fall
item.description: Follow Gigi the Giraffe on an extraordinary adventure to uncover the mystery of gravity — from falling apples to bending spacetime.

item.title: Gravity & Orbits
item.description: Create your own solar system — drag planets and watch gravity shape their orbits.

item.title: How Big Is the Universe?
item.description: A mind-expanding journey through the scale of the cosmos — from Earth to the observable universe.

item.title: How to Ace Your Physics Exam
item.description: Proven study strategies and problem-solving techniques for physics exams at any level.

item.title: Lumi the Firefly and the Secret Colors of the World
item.description: Lumi the Firefly embarks on a dazzling quest to understand light, color, and the wave nature of everything we see.

item.title: Newton's Cradle
item.description: Watch momentum transfer in action — pull back a ball and release to see Newton's laws at work.

item.title: Newton's Three Laws of Motion for Kids
item.description: A fun, animated explanation of Newton's three laws of motion — perfect for young scientists.

item.title: Nia the Ant and the Spark That Saved Willow Woods
item.description: Nia returns in an electrifying adventure where she must harness the power of electricity and magnetism to save her home.

item.title: Projectile Motion Simulator
item.description: Launch a projectile and explore how angle and velocity affect its path through the air.

item.title: Quantum Physics for Beginners
item.description: A gentle introduction to the strange world of quantum mechanics — superposition, entanglement, and more.

item.title: Tempo the Cheetah and the Fabric of Time
item.description: Tempo the Cheetah races at incredible speeds and discovers that time itself can stretch, space can bend, and the universe is far stranger than it appears.

item.title: The Tiny Ant Who Outsmart Them All
item.description: Join Nia the tiny ant as she uses the three laws of motion to outsmart bigger, stronger animals in the Great Race of Willow Woods.

item.title: Wave Interference Patterns
item.description: See how two wave sources create beautiful interference patterns of constructive and destructive waves.

item.title: Why Is the Sky Blue?
item.description: The physics behind one of the most common questions — explained with light, wavelengths, and Rayleigh scattering.

item.title: Zuki the Zebra and the Secret of Getting Things Done
item.description: Zuki the Zebra discovers that energy is everywhere — and that understanding work, power, and time is the secret to getting anything done.
